text: The surgeon carefully navigates within the gallbladder, aiming to prevent spillage of ICG dye. A vessel sealer is used to manage the thick gallbladder wall and minimize bleeding. The surgeon suctions out bile and ICG to identify the cystic duct entrance, which will guide further dissection and closure.

The surgeon approaches the cystic duct entrance, using a V-lock suture to close the gallbladder remnant area. Firefly cholangiogram and visual assessments are employed to ensure no leakage. A pedicle to falciform flap is created, followed by the application of Vista seal fiber and glue. A drain is placed at the conclusion of the procedure.

The surgeon attempts to close the top part of the gallbladder remnant, using the back wall if necessary, while being cautious of potential structures medially. The thick gallbladder wall is visible, aiding in the closure process.
